title: Job Finding Assistance * Substance Use Disorder Issues - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Employment > Job Finding Assistance * Substance Use Disorder Issues 6 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues Adults With Disabilities/Health Conditions African American Community Homelessness Issues Seasonal Workers Substance Use Disorder Issues Veterans Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF 4TH DIMENSION RECOVERY CENTER CLACKAMAS CENTER ADDICTION RECOVERY PEER MENTORING SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers peer mentoring services for substance use disorders. Mentors help clients to find housing, employment, applying for benefits such as TANF and OHP, guidance about legal aid programs, and transportation to recovery-oriented meetings and events. Also offers 12-step meetings and recovery-related activities and events, including dances, barbecues, poetry nights, and movie nights. 11690 SE 82nd Avenue Happy Valley, OR 97086 Eligibility: Individuals age 14-35 with substance use issues. Clients can be currently in treatment, just leaving treatment, or at any other stage of their recovery process. Hours: 7 days per week 2pm-10pm Email (971) 703-4623 Get Directions Visit Website More Details 4TH DIMENSION RECOVERY CENTER O'ROURKE CENTER ADDICTION RECOVERY PEER MENTORING SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers peer mentoring services for substance use disorders. Mentors help clients to find housing, employment, applying for benefits such as TANF and OHP, guidance about legal aid programs, and transportation to recovery-oriented meetings and events. Also offers 12-step meetings and recovery-related activities and events, including dances, barbecues, poetry nights, and movie nights. 362 SW Oak Street Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Individuals age 14-35 with substance use issues. Clients can be currently in treatment, just leaving treatment, or at any other stage of their recovery process. Hours: 7 days per week 2pm-10pm Email (971) 703-4623 Get Directions Visit Website More Details 4TH DIMENSION RECOVERY CENTER SMIT CENTER ADDICTION RECOVERY PEER MENTORING SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers peer mentoring services for substance use disorders. Mentors help clients to find housing, employment, applying for benefits such as TANF and OHP, guidance about legal aid programs, and transportation to recovery-oriented meetings and events. Also offers 12-step meetings and recovery-related activities and events, including dances, barbecues, poetry nights, and movie nights. 324 NE 9th Street Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Individuals age 14-35 with substance use issues. Clients can be currently in treatment, just leaving treatment, or at any other stage of their recovery process. Hours: 7 days per week 2pm-10pm Email (971) 703-4623 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BRIDGES TO CHANGE CLUB HOPE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a recovery drop-in center with support services for substance addiction, including support groups and an employment center. Additional services include laundry facilities, showers, and snacks. Services are available for individuals at any stage of recovery. 100 SE Cleveland Avenue Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Drop-In Center: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Showers and laundry: Monday-Friday 10am-3pm Get Directions Visit Website More Details OPTIONS FOR SOUTHERN OREGON INC THE HUB Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a recovery-oriented drop-in center where participants can sit, have a snack, and connect with others. Services include case management and information and referral to community resources, job finding assistance and employment support. Offers a variety of support groups that cover topics such as wellness, Medication Assisted Treatment (MAT) Education, and making connections. Assist with low-barrier access to treatment services and provides peer support for substance-related issues. Substance use and mental health assessments can be done by telehealth. Provides referrals for MAT. Distributes naloxone and offers education in partnership with other agencies. 404 SW 5th Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 9am-5pm, Wednesday 9am-12:30pm and 3pm-5pm (541) 916-5723 Get Directions Visit Website More Details RESTORED CONNECTIONS PEER CENTER SUPPORTED EMPLOYMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance in overcoming barriers to stable employment. Services available to clients may include basic resume assistance, connections with second chance employers, and assistance in preparing for interviews such as mock scenarios and role playing. Transportation to interviews and attire is provided to all participants in the program. 146 E 12th Avenue Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Individuals in recovery Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email (458) 544-0000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details 4TH DIMENSION RECOVERY CENTER ADDICTION RECOVERY PEER MENTORING SERVICES 4TH DIMENSION RECOVERY CENTER ADDICTION RECOVERY PEER MENTORING SERVICES 4TH DIMENSION RECOVERY CENTER ADDICTION RECOVERY PEER MENTORING SERVICES BRIDGES TO CHANGE CLUB HOPE OPTIONS FOR SOUTHERN OREGON INC THE HUB RESTORED CONNECTIONS PEER CENTER SUPPORTED EMPLOYMENT
